title: iron-session with cookieOptions.secure: false — the encrypted session cookie is sent over plain HTTP

params.passDetail: iron-session sets cookieOptions.secure to true — the encrypted session cookie is only ever transmitted over HTTPS.
params.failDetail: getIronSession is configured with cookieOptions: { secure: false }, so the sealed session cookie is sent over plain HTTP. On any non-TLS hop an attacker can capture the sealed cookie and replay it to impersonate the user until it expires. Set cookieOptions.secure: true in production.
params.absentCallDetail: No getIronSession call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: getIronSession does not set cookieOptions.secure; iron-session defaults secure to true in production — verify it is not overridden to false.

provenance.note: cookieOptions: { secure: false } is copied from local-dev config (no HTTPS) into production, sending the sealed session cookie in the clear where it can be sniffed and replayed.
